summary.intent: After pushing PR #732 (Tabs adapter API split into primitives and styled templates), the user wanted the assistant to properly execute the repo's `waiting-for-codex-review` skill — polling until the automated Codex reviewer posted a verdict, then addressing any findings with TDD rigor before handing back.
summary.outcome: Codex posted 6 unresolved threads (4 Dioxus reactivity/context staleness issues, 2 Leptos styled-wrapper API parity gaps). All were addressed with regression tests written first, production fixes applied, and a fix commit pushed. Thread reply/resolve and the final `@codex review` re-trigger were the next pending steps at transcript end.

repo: Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a top-level PR comment; it does not reliably auto-trigger on push. The reaction contract is: 👀 = reviewing, 👍 = approved, 👀-drops-with-no-👍 = findings posted.
The skill at `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md` mandates staying in the polling loop until 👍 — treating PR publication + 👀 appearance as a handoff point is explicitly wrong.

Preferred poll mechanism is Bash `run_in_background` (single terminal-verdict notification + `Read` of stdout file), not Monitor (best-effort per-event stream that can silently drop the terminal event).

Dioxus browser tests run as: `WASM_BINDGEN_TEST_ONLY_WEB=1 cargo test -p ars-dioxus --features web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --test tabs_wasm`; native: `cargo test -p ars-dioxus --test tabs`; Leptos SSR: `cargo test -p ars-leptos-components --features ssr --test tabs`.
Repo CI gate is `cargo xci-fast`; formatter is `cargo xfmt`.
Panels in the Dioxus Tabs implementation stay mounted as empty containers rather than being removed from the DOM — presence assertions must check panel body content, not `role=tabpanel` count.

code.finding: TabShell and CloseTrigger derived attrs statically at mount; they needed to subscribe reactively to the machine so selected-state and aria-label changes propagate after selection or provider-message updates.
Close aria-label memo tracked the initial row label only; it needed to reference the refreshed row label signal so localization message changes propagate to close triggers.
Custom close glyph is configured via `.close_trigger(...)`, not `.closable(...)` — the two builder methods are distinct.

Leptos styled wrapper did not expose `value` (controlled selection) or `on_reorder`. Optional props with `Option<T>` cannot be forwarded with view-macro shorthand — the fix branches and omits the prop when absent rather than passing `None`.
Same `value`/`on_reorder` parity gap and same optional-prop forwarding constraint as the CSS variant.

Leptos component builder omits optional callback props by not including the attribute at all — passing `None::<CallbackType>` is invalid `view!` macro syntax and causes a parse error, not a type error.

workflow: GraphQL queries for PR review threads are fragile to extra closing braces; prefer minimal/flat queries and validate syntax before a long polling loop.
TDD sequence for review findings: write the failing test that reproduces the exact observable regression, confirm red, then apply the narrowest production fix, confirm green.
After every fix push in the Codex review loop, post `@codex review` explicitly — auto-retrigger from a push is unreliable.

Cargo's test binary accepts only one `--test <name>` filter per invocation; running the whole test file and filtering by grep in output is the practical workaround when multiple new tests need simultaneous red/green verification.

Defensive re-check after long quiet periods (>10 min): `Read` the background task's stdout file first, then re-fetch reactions with `gh api .../reactions` — the notification stream can silently drop terminal events.

friction: Assistant initially entered a manual `sleep 120` polling loop instead of running the skill-prescribed `run_in_background` bash script — required user intervention to correct.
Multiple GraphQL review-thread queries failed due to extra closing braces, wasting several round trips before switching to a minimal query.
Dioxus test construction used `.closable()` instead of `.close_trigger()` for the custom-close-glyph case, requiring a test fix before the red/green cycle could proceed.
Leptos `view!` macro silently rejects `None::<T>` for optional props with a parse error rather than a type error, making the failure mode non-obvious.
Leptos optional-prop shorthand forwarding (`value=value`) does not compile when the outer wrapper holds `Option<T>` — required a branch/omit pattern instead.
Dioxus lazy-mount test assertion required correction: the 'panel is gone' check needed to query body content rather than `role=tabpanel` presence, because panels remain mounted as empty shells.
open_items: Inline replies to all 6 Codex threads (with commit SHA + one-line summary) and GraphQL resolution of each thread have not been posted yet — required before re-triggering Codex.
`@codex review` re-trigger comment on PR #732 has not been posted yet — the loop will not advance until this is done.
CI jobs on PR #732 had some pending checks at the last snapshot; final CI green state was not confirmed.
No Dioxus-components styled Tabs tests exist yet (only Leptos-components were added this session) — parity gap remains.
